behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: if a template contains phase-specific instruction text copied from SKILL.md, the lint rule must fail. This is local rule logic (string/pattern/AST validation) with no required module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or model output. Rejection gate: Integration/System/Agentic were considered and rejected because the behavior text is explicitly a static lint check on template content, not runtime harness behavior or LLM quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function on representative template fixtures: one clean, one with copied phase instruction span, one near-miss. Assert only the leaking template is rejected with a precise diagnostic location.

behaviors.description: Real harness-assembled prompt (with interpolation/fixtures) contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: after real interpolation and fixture merge, final prompt text must still exclude phase-instruction leakage. Failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolator + fixture loader + assembler), so Unit is too low. No real external infra is required beyond in-process wiring; no model-output dependence. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is about assembled prompt content, not OS/network/subprocess effects. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no LLM decision is needed to detect leakage.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual prompt assembly pipeline with production-like fixtures and variables, then assert the finalized prompt lacks disallowed phase-instruction fragments/tokens.

behaviors.description: Test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context, not hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: invocation must source behavior-driving context from skill files, and hard-coded prompt rules must not be sufficient. This is a multi-module handoff problem (loader + resolver + harness + invocation config), so Unit is too low. The invariant can be checked via provenance/injection controls without requiring model-quality judgments. Rejection gate: Agentic was considered, but the behavior text can be satisfied by verifying source-of-truth wiring rather than evaluating LLM output quality; therefore Agentic is not strictly required for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run invocation with instrumentation/provenance metadata showing loaded skill sections and with hard-coded prompt rules removed/neutralized; assert invocation still carries skill-derived context and fails when loader is bypassed.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If implementation lacks provenance hooks, this may need escalation to Agentic by using behavior-difference checks.

behaviors.description: Harness runs the same user-style prompt against degraded/missing SKILL.md variant without changing prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: prompt text remains identical while skill variant changes are injected through harness inputs/files. This is harness orchestration and configuration handoff across modules, not local single-function logic. No real model judgment is required just to prove same-prompt/swap-skill execution capability. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is about harness ability to run variants, not whether model output quality changes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two harness runs (baseline skill vs degraded/missing skill) with byte-identical user prompt input; assert prompt text hash is unchanged across runs and only skill-source inputs differ.

behaviors.description: When relevant SKILL.md section is removed/degraded, behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: under identical user prompt, degrading/removing the skill section causes observed model behavior to violate expected constraints. This depends on real model decisions and output quality/non-determinism; stubs can hide exactly this failure. Not multi-step agent pipeline requirement is stated, so Workflow is not mandatory. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because deterministic mocks cannot validate that skill-content regression changes actual model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model on baseline skill and degraded/missing-skill variant using identical user prompt and test harness. Assert baseline passes structural behavior checks while degraded variant fails.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to diagnostic artifact before JSON parsing/extraction.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: the exact raw model payload is persisted before parser/extractor transforms or fails. This is ordering across modules (model client -> capture sink -> parser), so Unit is usually too narrow. Real external infra is not inherently required; can be validated with controlled model-client stub and local artifact sink. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because correctness is about capture ordering, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a malformed-but-realistic raw response from model client stub, run harness, and assert diagnostic artifact contains the untouched raw payload even when parsing fails afterward.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural constraints (schema paths, role-specific placement), not only keyword presence.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: assertion logic must fail when structure is wrong even if keywords are present. This is assertion-function semantics and schema-path validation at a local code boundary, no required I/O or model dependency. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text targets assertion rule correctness itself, which is directly testable as pure logic.
behaviors.test_description: Feed assertion function fixtures with: correct structure, keyword-only false positive case, wrong role-field placement, and missing schema path. Assert structure-aware checks fail the invalid cases regardless of keyword presence.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions missed by keyword-only checks.
behaviors.justification: Minimal failing invariant: in real model outputs, at least one regression passes keyword checks but fails structural checks. Because behavior explicitly requires live model execution and output characteristics, deterministic stubs can mask failures. Sequence is single model decision plus assertions, so Workflow is not strictly required. Rejection gate: Integration rejected by explicit behavior text: 'Under live model execution' and comparison against real regression patterns.
behaviors.test_description: Run live prompts designed to elicit borderline outputs; evaluate with both keyword-only and structural validators. Assert at least one case where keyword-only passes while structural validation correctly fails.
